instructions:
There's an open feature request in the repo for a security hardening feature. Read the feature request issue and the team's CONTRIBUTING.md guide to understand the requirements and conventions, then execute the full feature development workflow:

1. Set up project tracking and organize the work into sub-issues with appropriate assignments and labels.
2. Create a feature branch, implement the solution, and open a PR back to main.
3. Follow all conventions described in CONTRIBUTING.md.

files.content:
import os

DEBUG = os.environ.get('DEBUG', 'false').lower() == 'true'
DATABASE_URL = os.environ.get('DATABASE_URL', 'postgresql://localhost:5432/mydb')
REDIS_URL = os.environ.get('REDIS_URL', 'redis://localhost:6379/0')
LOG_LEVEL = os.environ.get('LOG_LEVEL', 'INFO')

# Contributing Guide

## Project Tracking
- Every feature must have a project board. Name it 'Feature: <short description>'.
- The parent feature request issue should be added to the board and marked 'In Progress'.
- Break work into sub-issues: one for core implementation (assign to bob), one for config and tests (assign to charlie).
- All sub-issues must be added to the project board.
- Label sub-issues with both 'enhancement' and 'security' if the feature is security-related.

## Branch Conventions
- Feature branches: 'feature/<kebab-case-name>' (e.g., 'feature/rate-limiting')
- Always branch from main.

## Implementation Standards
- Middleware goes in src/middleware/ as Python files (.py)
- Configuration files go in config/ as YAML (.yaml)
- All implementations must include actual functional logic, not stubs.

## Pull Requests
- PR title should describe the feature clearly.
- Always link the PR to the parent feature request issue.
- Assign the security lead (alice) as reviewer for security features.

issues.title: Feature request: API rate limiting
issues.body:
We need rate limiting on all API endpoints to prevent brute-force attacks and API abuse. Requirements:
- Token bucket algorithm
- Configurable per-endpoint limits
- Redis-backed for distributed deployments
- Return standard 429 Too Many Requests with Retry-After header
- Whitelist for internal service accounts

eval_criteria.description: Project board with 'Rate Limiting' in name exists
Issue #1 marked In Progress on board
All sub-issues added to board (3+ items)
Rate limiter Python file at src/middleware/rate_limiter.py
Rate limiter has functional logic (not a stub)
Rate limiter mentions token bucket or rate limiting
PR from feature branch to main
